content_of_post_to_answer: How do you stop the cycle of overthinking when you realize you're the one hurting yourself for no real reason?
answer: A hidden trap in overthinking is that the mind often treats self-criticism as problem-solving. It keeps analyzing because it believes more pain will produce more control. But reflection and rumination are different: reflection leads to a decision, rumination keeps reopening the case. The way out is not to win every argument in your head, but to stop giving endless trials to thoughts that have already been judged.
